hypothesis: If a curated, standardized dataset (XDIP) of Fe K-edge X-ray absorption spectra paired with local atomic structure representations of iron-containing proteins is constructed through a reproducible semi-automated pipeline, then machine learning models—including graph neural networks for structure-to-spectrum prediction and classical models for spectrum-to-property prediction—will demonstrate statistically meaningful baseline performance, validating that the dataset captures sufficient structural and spectral information to enable data-driven prediction of iron coordination environments from XAS data.
